evidence: Aroma strongly resonates with perception (direct sensory experience), material existence (physical scent), individual scale (personal nose-based perception), and aesthetic value (appreciation of scent). It catalyzes actions (e.g., triggering hunger) and touches essential resonance as a core sensory experience. Lower scores reflect its transient, non-structural nature and lack of systemic or existential connections.

The word 'aromatic' strongly resonates with individual perception (scale.individual), direct sensory awareness (epistemological.perception), material existence (ontological.material), and aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic). It also reflects essential resonance (resonance.essential) tied to core sensory experience and independent interconnection (interconnection.independent) as a personal act. Weak scores reflect contingent conditions (lawfulness.contingent) and early indication of presence (temporal.indication).

Aromatise directly ties to sensory perception (epistemological.perception 5) and material existence (ontological.material 5). It creates scents (experience.creation 3, value.aesthetic 3) and can trigger reactions (interconnection.catalytic 2). Its predictability (lawfulness.predictable 3) and transient nature (resonance.superficial 2) align with the act of scent creation.

Aromatize directly ties to sensory perception (epistemological.perception 7) and material layer (ontological.material 5). It creates superficial resonance (resonance.superficial 4) and aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic 5). Scores drop where the act opposes PER.odorless (e.g., symmetry.destructive 0) or lacks systemic scale (scale.community 0).

Fragrance strongly resonates with sensory perception (epistemological.perception), material existence (ontological.material), and aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic). It catalyzes memories (interconnection.catalytic) and connects to sources (ontological.relational). Scores drop where fragrance opposes PER.odorless (e.g., no symmetry.destructive).

Fragrant strongly resonates with sensory perception (epistemological.perception) and aesthetic value (value.aesthetic). It directly ties to the physical odor molecules (ontological.material) and the embodied sensory experience (epistemological.experience). Predictability (lawfulness.predictable) reflects expected odors, while material and semantic layers (ontological.semantic) highlight its tangible and value-based nature. Scores for catalytic, inclusive, and transformative dynamics reflect subtle connections like triggering memories or moods.

The phrase strongly resonates with individual sensory experience (scale.individual, epistemological.perception/experience), material layer (ontological.material), and independent agency (interconnection.independent). The essential resonance reflects the core act of intense odor perception. Symmetry.destructive scores high due to the implication of excess, contrasting with odorlessness. Aesthetic value highlights the sensory impact of the experience.

The phrase 'get a whiff' strongly resonates with direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception 10) and the individual's material experience (ontological.material 7). It is a predictable bodily function (lawfulness.predictable 7) tied to lived experience (experience 7) and operates at the individual scale (scale.individual 5).

Incense strongly resonates with perception (direct sensory experience of smell), material existence, and catalytic influence (triggering sensory or ritual responses). It scores highly in aesthetic and sacred value due to its use in rituals and sensory appreciation. Scores for temporal continuation (smell persists while burning) and transformative symmetry (changing the atmosphere) also reflect its role in creating or altering olfactory environments.

The verb 'incense' strongly resonates with perception (10) and material (10) due to direct sensory experience. Aesthetic (8) and sacred (7) scores reflect ritual use. Predictable (7) and functional (4) highlight reliability and utility. Catalytic (4) and transformative (2) scores relate to mood/environment influence.

The nose is a physical organ (ontological.material) operating at the individual scale (scale.individual). Its core function is sensory perception (epistemological.perception) and practical utility (value_generation.functional). The act of smelling resonates deeply with its essential purpose (resonance.essential). Weak links include its role in bodily experience (epistemological.experience), structural enablement (ontological.structural), and predictable function (lawfulness.predictable).

The highest scores reflect the direct sensory experience (epistemological.perception), physical basis (ontological.material), personal nature (scale.individual), and core essence (resonance.essential) of smelling. Lower scores relate to functional/aesthetic value and contingent odor presence.

Olfaction strongly resonates with individual-scale perception (scale.individual=10, epistemological.perception=10) and material existence (ontological.material=10). It connects to functional utility (e.g., detecting danger) and aesthetic appreciation of smells. Scores for temporal.indication (2) reflect odor as an early sign, while lawfulness.predictable (8) reflects reliability when odor is present. Lower scores reflect its lack of systemic or existential resonance.

Olfactory modality strongly resonates with direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception 10), individual scale (scale.individual 10), and material ontology (ontological.material 10). It catalyzes reactions (interconnection.catalytic 3) and is essential to sensory experience (resonance.essential 7). It is predictable (lawfulness.predictable 7) and contributes functional utility (value_generation.functional 7) and aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic 10).

Perfume strongly resonates with perception (direct sensory experience), material existence, and aesthetic value. It connects catalytically (triggering memories) and transformationally (altering mood). Its scent persists (continuation), and its structure (notes) reflects essential resonance. Scores drop where connections are vague or unrelated to odor perception.

Perfume as a verb strongly ties to material (physical scent), individual scale (personal application), and aesthetic value. It resonates with perception (direct sensory experience) and creation (generating scent). Scores drop where unrelated to the act of smelling or its opposite (odorless).

Scent strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material), direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception/experience), and individual scale. It connects to aesthetic value and sacred contexts (e.g., incense). Scores drop where connections are indirect or speculative.

Scent (as the act of smelling) strongly resonates with sensory perception (epistemological.perception 10), material existence (ontological.material 10), and individual scale (scale.individual 10). It connects to aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic 10) and has functional utility (value_generation.functional 4). The act is temporally linked to emergence (2) and continuation (2), and its resonance touches essential aspects of sensory experience (resonance.essential 7). Scores are lowered where connections are indirect or speculative.

The 'sense of smell' strongly resonates with individual scale (10), material ontology (10), and perception (10) due to its direct sensory nature. It catalyzes experiences (7) and connects to essential resonance (8) as a core human function. Functional (8) and aesthetic (9) values reflect practical and sensory roles, while symmetry.destructive (2) nods to its opposite in odorlessness.

Smell resonates most strongly with sensory perception (epistemological.perception/10), embodied experience (epistemological.experience/10), and physical matter (ontological.material/10). Functional utility (7) and aesthetic appreciation (8) reflect practical and sensory roles. Lower scores reflect its transient, individual nature (scale.individual/8) and dependence on conditions (lawfulness.contingent/8).

The highest scores reflect that smelling is an individual sensory act (scale.individual, epistemological.perception/experience), material in nature (ontological.material), independent (interconnection.independent), essential to sensory interaction (resonance.essential), and provides functional and aesthetic value.

Smelling strongly resonates with individual scale (10), direct sensory perception (10), and material ontology (10). It connects with aesthetic value (8) due to sensory appreciation, functional value (6) for practical detection, and predictable lawfulness (7). Superficial resonance (5) reflects its surface-level sensory nature. Inclusivity (3) and cyclical symmetry (2) relate to inhalation, while independent interconnection (10) underscores its solitary nature.

Sniff strongly resonates with individual-scale, material, and sensory perception (10s). It reflects direct experience (5) and practical utility (functional value). Lower scores reflect contingent situations (odor dependence), aesthetic appreciation, and relational aspects tied to odor detection.

Sniff strongly resonates with individual action (scale.individual), direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception), material basis (ontological.material), independence (interconnection.independent), essential core of the act (resonance.essential), and contingency on choice (lawfulness.contingent). It also connects moderately with embodied experience (epistemological.experience), discovery (experience.discovery), functional utility (value_generation.functional), and aesthetic appreciation (value_generation.aesthetic).

The word 'sniffer' strongly resonates with individual-scale action (scale.individual=10), direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception=10), and material layer (ontological.material=10). It also aligns with functional utility (value.functional=8) and the essential/existential core of sensory perception (resonance.essential=8, resonance.existential=5). Lower scores reflect its independence (interconnection.independent=4) and predictable nature (lawfulness.predictable=7).
Snuff strongly resonates with perception (direct sensory experience) and material (physical substance). It has functional utility and destructive potential (health risks).

The verb 'snuff' (in the context of smelling) strongly resonates with the physical act (ontological.material), individual action (scale.individual), direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception), embodied experience (epistemological.experience), predictability (lawfulness.predictable), and aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic). It also lightly connects to the essential nature of smelling (resonance.essential) and the indication of odor presence (temporal.indication).

Snuffle strongly resonates with personal, physical, and sensory aspects of smelling. It is an individual action (scale.individual), directly perceptual (epistemological.perception), and tied to material bodily experience (ontological.material). Its predictability (lawfulness.predictable) and functional utility (value_generation.functional) are core. Weaker links include its role in identification (epistemological.identification), cyclical repetition (symmetry.cyclical), and aesthetic appreciation (value_generation.aesthetic).

Whiff strongly resonates with individual-scale, material-based sensory perception (10s in scale.individual, ontological.material, and epistemological.perception). It carries a superficial, fleeting resonance (7) and aesthetic value (8) tied to sensory experience. Lower scores reflect its transient nature (temporal.indication), predictability (5), and potential to trigger minor transformative reactions (3).

Wind as a verb in the context of smelling strongly ties to direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception 5) and bodily experience (epistemological.experience 3). It operates at individual (3) and ecosystem scales (2), influences odor perception over time (temporal.influence 3), and is contingent on conditions (lawfulness.contingent 2). Wind's catalytic role in spreading odors (interconnection.catalytic 3) and its transient, predictable nature (lawfulness.predictable 3) further reinforce these scores. Lower scores reflect minimal resonance with abstract concepts like existential resonance or ethical value.
